title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 111.
content:
There was an Old Man, on whose nose,
    Most birds of the air could repose;
    But they all flew away,
    At the closing of day,
    Which relieved that Old Man and his nose.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 111.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
